title: Hemoglobin test interpretation
summary: Hemoglobin levels are used to assess anemia risk, but the normal range depends on the laboratory and patient factors.
content: Hemoglobin is an iron-rich protein in red blood cells that carries oxygen. Elevated or low values can support a broader anemia or blood disorder workup, but the result must be interpreted against the laboratory reference range and the patient's age, sex, and clinical context.

provenance.curation_method: Manual curation from MedlinePlus medical test guidance.
provenance.evidence_basis: Reference-range interpretation and anemia screening context.
provenance.curation_notes: Demo-grade grounding entry for CBC interpretation.

applicability.intended_use: Ground extracted hemoglobin indicators and explain why interpretation depends on reference ranges.
applicability.population_notes: Adult-oriented demo content. Race, sex, and age can affect reference interpretation.
applicability.limitations_summary: Reference range varies by laboratory and patient factors.

limitations: Lab-specific reference ranges still govern final interpretation.
This entry does not diagnose anemia by itself.
Pediatric or pregnancy-specific interpretation requires additional sources.
